div.head: The Ambassador in Germany (Schurman) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
194. During a visit of WileyJohn C. Wiley, First Secretary of Embassy. to Chief of American Division of Foreign Office on Saturday,October 12, 1929. De Haas suggested that Wiley see Trautmann,
Chief Far Eastern Division, who gave him text of note telegraphed by German Government on October 7 to Chinese and Soviet Governments proposing cessation of arrests of their respective nationals and reciprocal release of those already arrested.The Moscow Izvestia on October 18 announced the Soviet Government’s refusal to accept the proposal. Trautmann expressed the hope that the Department would adopt a friendly attitude toward the project. The support of the American press too he said would be most helpful.
Text of noteNot printed. by next pouch.
